item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
replaced the image with author's permission

it is sourced as follows:

Filename: tgifridays.png
Name: tgi fridays
Author: Betty Longbottom
License: CC BY-SA 2.0
Source Link: TGI Fridays - Birstall Retail Park

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've never eaten at a TGI Friday even though I lived in Texas for like 16 years with one about ten minutes from my house

I imagine eating there is vaguely like this scp

+1

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: As the original image has been deleted, it is to be assumed that the image is no longer cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Somehow, I don't think ETHCOM would allow enhanced interrogation of a non-hostile, humanoid being.
That's pretty neat

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well written and just like this one from S&CPlastics leaves no clear answer, just a shrug and that's just it. Ending with Agent casually getting snack is essential to that feeling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’d get some mozzarella sticks if the universe ended too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I don't know what a TGI Friday's is, likely due to not being american, but I feel like it matters very little.

The end of everything, all comes together in a mediocore food place, with bored workers, around the mill manager and yeah.

What I guess im trying to say is;

It's so dumb,
I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the crushing nihilism in this piece
and I wasn't sure if it was gonna be a Hitchhiker's Guide reference til the very end. might have felt a bit shoehorned in for other people, but I enjoyed that

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview is a bit over-long, which ruins the sparse humour of it somewhat. But the whole still just about holds up for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you could've done without the exposition on what the anomaly represents, which I feel could've been cut or delivered in a far better manner, but this still gave me a good enough sense of existential… dread? Gloominess? I don't know what the right word but regardless that was a good enough sense to make me enjoy this.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Capitalism just chews up the universe and spits out a bland chain restaurant.

Seems about right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm also finding myself in agreement with Cyan here. The concept is OK, in my opinion, but I feel like it could be explained better. I found myself somewhat confused coming out of the Description.

I dislike the fact that most of the exposition is in the interview. In addition, a lot of the dialog seems somewhat rant-y, and I found myself skimming it.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's refreshing to see a scip that drops making you shit yourself so it can making you feel like shit instead. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is something about the dialogue here that reminds me of the second chapter from The Grapes of Wrath. The setting is dull, and the characters are aware of that. Granted, the second chapter of that book is probably one of the few I found to be captivating, so take this as a compliment.

While I'm a bit disappointed that it ended up not going in Rat's Nest, I might be able to change that with a tale ;)

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow this is the most depressing scp I have ever read. A fucking TGI Fridays.

Heres yalls plus 1. You bastards.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: TGI fridays gay confirmed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed with all of the above… the dialogue was well-written enough that I couldn't downvote, but there just isn't enough here. It doesn't help that Agent Ruth's musings are so glib, though that might be the intention (I do like the Manager's response of "yeah, sure, if you say so").

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with this. It's the Restaurant at the End of the Universe, but more boring and miserable, by design.

I enjoy this kind of humour and tone, but the substance of the article is just two people talking about how life is a bit shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The CSS is hideous. Downvoted before I could even read a word.

ETA: Having finished, there's really not all that much to this. The conversation is mildly amusing in places, but, much like the scenario presented, feels pointless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not a fan of this one. The exposition is all done directly in that log, and it's just the Foundation character stating what we already could have inferred from concept alone, leaving the whole thing feeling hollow. The Restaurant at the End of the Universe vibes and the Pan Galactic Gargle Blaster reference didn't do much other than make me think 'yeah, this is similar to some stuff' but there's not enough original stuff here to make it mean anything. The concept is already dependent on a real company's existence, and it is quite literally a Restaurant at the End of the Universe. Same concept, except multiples instead of one that uses time travel like in Hitchhiker's.

The logo's use doesn't seem to have an in-universe explanation, which does give it a -J feel even though the concept (including its humor) doesn't have to feel like a -J. Also, the CSS does not change the Wikidot account info text in the top right to a darker color, the logo is pixelated as hell (I'm sure my monitor resolution is to blame for that, though, but high-res monitors are becoming more and more common and it doesn't really take that much longer a load time to have a reasonably large image, especially if it takes up that much of the screen), and the completely blank header space is visually unappealing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pan-Galactic Peach Long Island Iced Tea

+42

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is awesome, depressing, kinda funny, everything great about skips im ome neat little package. Even caught the Hitchhikers reference at the end. Although this could all just be a nod to the restaurant at the end of the universe…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I have to give this a no vote for now because questions like these eat at me. And I have to imagine the foundation would do a more…thorough interrogation and tests…I guess those tests exist and just aren't shown here, but with only one interview and no tests, the casualness of the interview is jarring.

That said its also wonderful and hilarious so I wouldn't dream of downvoting. have my enthusiastic +0 i guess?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been having my doubts about Series V as of late, but this just casts all of them out to sea and gives me hope's for the series now

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Premise is both nihilistic and fun in just the right amounts. Leaves me with a few questions unanswered123456, so I wouldn't exactly mind if this was elaborated in some way or another, but I still like it as it stands, so take your upvote.

Footnotes
1. Is the TGI Friday's the only space left in the universe after it collapses?
2. What is the threshold where this starts to happen? Does this only happen after heat-death, XKs, information deletion via black holes, etc?
3. Were the employees created when the first extra-universal TGI Friday's was, or were they just people in the universe who were shoved into the restaurant when it died?
4. Is the law of conservation of mass intact in the remnants of a dead universe, or is everything gone save for what was needed for the TGI Fridays and the food/drinks it makes?
5. Without a reliable and infinite way to measure time, how do the employees handle shifts, eating, and sleeping?
6. I strongly believe that this article would fit nicely into a canon, but I can't decide which one would work best. Part of me really wants this to get added to S&C Plastics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The CSS was great! Also thank you for reminding me to credit you for it in the post, I knew I was forgetting something <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't just like this, I love this. I don't have any words, this is just a great scip. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is hilarious and depressing at the same time. And somehow works really well. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thanks for using my logo, even if the CSS was sloppy. loved this in draft, love it now. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hit +1 and it went from 0 to 5. I salute you, OP.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
A big thanks to my mother this time for helping with this idea!

Also big thanks to my co-author DolphinSlugchugger for both the photoshop, the name, lots of idea-work and the dialogue for this skip!

Thanks to my critters, plaguebearer, Captain Kirby, LordStonefish, kinchtheknifeblade, Uncle Nicolini, Hot-coles, and anyone else I missed!

Enjoy what wasn't an 001 Proposal, was briefly an 001 Proposal, and then wasn't again.

Also big thanks to Plague Bearer for the Logo used in the TGI Foundation CSS <3

Edit: Image source: https://www.flickr.com/photos/paulsimaging/6726689615

Edit 2: CSS removed due to more than enough people voicing negative opinions about it, will return if need be
